title: Islamic Psychology _ Human Behaviour and Experience
book: Islamic Psychology _ Human Behaviour and Experience

node_types.description: A historical or contemporary individual relevant to Islamic psychology, including scholars, therapists, clients, and role models.

properties.name: full_name
properties.description: The person's full name

properties.name: role
properties.description: The person's role or title (e.g., scholar, therapist, client, Prophet, companion)

properties.name: contributions
properties.description: Summary of the person's contributions to Islamic psychology or related fields

properties.name: era
properties.description: Historical period or dates associated with the person

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 Islamic psychology: Context, definitions and perspectives
Chapter 2 A brief history of Islamic psychology: Origins and heritage
Chapter 3 Perspectives on human nature
Chapter 9 Personality development
Chapter 24 The anatomy of Islamic psychotherapy

node_types.description: A concept, value, or principle from Islamic tradition relevant to psychology, health, or therapy.

properties.name: concept_name
properties.description: Name of the Islamic concept (e.g., Fitrah, Tazkiyat al-nafs, Tawheed, Taqwa, Nafs)

properties.name: definition
properties.description: Definition or explanation of the concept

properties.name: category
properties.description: Category (e.g., spiritual, ethical, legal, psychological)

properties.name: scriptural_sources
properties.description: Qur’anic verses or Hadith references supporting the concept

node_types.description: A psychological construct or model relevant to human behaviour, mental health, or therapy.

properties.name: construct_name
properties.description: Name of the psychological construct or model (e.g., attachment, motivation, memory, personality trait, locus of control)

properties.name: definition
properties.description: Definition or explanation of the construct

properties.name: category
properties.description: Category (e.g., cognitive, affective, behavioural, developmental)

properties.name: related_theories
properties.description: Associated psychological theories or frameworks

node_types.description: A therapeutic or counselling approach, model, or intervention relevant to Islamic or integrative mental health care.

properties.name: approach_name
properties.description: Name of the therapeutic approach or model (e.g., Islamic CBT, Ruqyah, traditional healing, integrative psychotherapy)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Description of the approach, including its principles and methods

properties.name: evidence_base
properties.description: Summary of empirical support or validation

properties.name: applicable_contexts
properties.description: Contexts or conditions where the approach is used

node_types.description: A mental, physical, or spiritual health condition relevant to Islamic psychology and clinical care.

properties.name: condition_name
properties.description: Name of the health condition (e.g., depression, anxiety, addiction, disability, aggression, sleep disorder)

properties.description: Category (e.g., mental, physical, spiritual, developmental)

properties.description: Description of the condition, including symptoms and diagnostic criteria

properties.name: islamic_perspective
properties.description: Islamic views or considerations regarding the condition

node_types.description: A competency, skill, or value required for therapists or counsellors working in Islamic or culturally aligned contexts.
